title: CodeAutonomy Evaluation Result
description: Latest structured Evaluator result. Validation.md remains the human/agent-readable cumulative history.

summary.description: Human-readable one-line summary of the latest result.

failedChecks.description: Failed or blocked acceptance checks. Empty when result=pass.

category.description: Standard failure category used by orchestrator and future policy logic.

triageSource.description: Model-first triage signal for failure classification. code_deterministic = classified by known pattern with no model review needed. requires_model_review = the code could not classify and the Evaluator must re-examine the evidence. model_reviewed = the Evaluator reviewed and re-classified this entry.

needsModelReview.description: Explicit signal to the Evaluator/Generator. true means this entry was not classified by code and requires model analysis. false means either the code classified it or the Evaluator has reviewed it.

evidence.description: Artifacts or command evidence from this evaluation round.

verificationUnblockChanges.description: Temporary harness/config/test-hook changes used only to unblock verification. All items must be restored or promoted before finish.

status.description: active cannot be accepted by completion-check.

evaluatorChanges.description: Explicit declaration of every file the Evaluator modified during this round. Generator owns product/runtime-code repair; Evaluator may only touch verifier/probe-flow/test-harness/evidence files. Any item with category=product_code or with files outside the allowed scope must trigger nextAction=retry_generator and is rejected by the completion gate.

category.description: product_code is reserved as an explicit illegal-self-report value. The completion gate uses it to detect Evaluator boundary violations and route to retry_generator.

temporaryVerificationChanges.description: Deprecated alias for `verificationUnblockChanges`. Will be removed in a future release; emit a deprecation warning when normalized.

nextAction.description: Control signal consumed by orchestrator. result=pass requires nextAction=finish. `stop` is treated as a synonym of `stop_blocked` (non-recoverable hard stop). `stop_blocked` marks a non-recoverable failure that should not be retried (signing/policy/destructive guard, etc.). `pause_for_external` pauses the task for an external dependency (device, signing, third-party service) and parks the task as `human_input_pending` so it can be resumed later.

askUserQuestion.description: Structured question for human decision when the evaluator cannot safely choose a path automatically. CodeAutonomy treats `nextAction=ask_user` as a hard interruption of the autonomous full_auto loop, so it must only be used for one of the whitelisted categories below. For screenshot-based visual fallback, include screenshot/evidence paths, expected claim, and explicit confirmation options.

category.description: Hard-interrupt category. Only these values may trigger nextAction=ask_user: unauthorized_destructive_or_sensitive (privilege escalation, account/privacy/legal, large-scale data deletion, force-push, signing key rotation), system_or_external_dependency (system SDK/runtime install, Docker/database services, browser drivers, private registry credentials, OS-level changes), real_device_or_signing (device unlock/Developer Mode/USB debugging/UDID, code-signing/provisioning/team selection, manual install on physical device), manual_visual_confirmation (final visual claim that pure measurement plus AI Visual Review still cannot prove), repeated_same_failure (same error/category fails N times in a row and CodeAutonomy cannot break the loop without a human decision).

riskTier.description: Model self-assessment that is the PRIMARY judge of whether this ask_user is a self-serviceable soft pause or a genuine hard gate. CodeAutonomy biases toward automation and trusts this label over keyword heuristics. `safe_self_service`: CodeAutonomy can resolve it itself (long build/compile, minimal reversible verification-unblock patch on code/script/wrapper/build-config, re-signing with the user's own certificates / automatic signing, in-app device driving, retryable env tweak) -- the completion gate rewrites the ask_user back to retry_generator so the loop keeps running. `sensitive_hard_gate`: a genuine human decision is required (irreversible/destructive action such as delete/wipe/reset/force-push, account/credential/keychain login, payment, or a real device/permission gate) -- the loop is allowed to pause. Signing/certificate selection alone is NOT a hard gate. Omit only when you cannot self-classify; then CodeAutonomy falls back to conservative keyword heuristics.

reversible.description: Optional reinforcement of riskTier: true when the action CodeAutonomy would take is fully reversible (checkpoint/restore/promote), false when it is irreversible or account/security-affecting.

selfServiceRationale.description: Short justification for the chosen riskTier, e.g. why a patch is reversible (recorded in verificationUnblockChanges and restored before finish) or why a gate is a genuine hard interrupt.

userAuthorized.description: Set true ONLY when the action you would otherwise pause for is already authorized by the user via the pre-implementation `runtime-state.json.planner.preImplementationReview.decisionBundle.destructiveActionsAllowList`. When true (and the allow list is non-empty), the completion gate trusts that the user already consented and rewrites the ask_user back to retry_generator so the loop proceeds without re-asking. You own the semantic match between the pending action and what the user authorized; never fabricate consent. Whether to pause is ultimately the user's call (user intent is the final arbiter).

defaultAction.description: What CodeAutonomy should do if the human does not answer. Usually stop/replan.

qualityChecks.description: Functional-adjacent quality checks merged after the main evaluator: performance, UX/smoothness, stability, architecture, maintainability.

triageSource.description: Model-first triage signal. code_deterministic = classified by known pattern with no model review needed. requires_model_review = the code could not classify and the Evaluator must re-examine the evidence. model_reviewed = the Evaluator reviewed and re-classified this entry.

needsModelReview.description: Explicit signal to the Evaluator/Generator. true means this entry was not classified by code and requires model analysis. false means either the code classified it or the Evaluator has reviewed it.

testCaseId.description: Source TestCases.md ID when this quality check maps to a declared testcase.

qualityCategory.description: Batching category for quality testcase execution.

skipReason.description: Reason if a functional regression or quality rerun was safely skipped.

testResults.description: Per-TestCases.md result matrix for this evaluation round. Required TC-* cases must pass before nextAction=finish is accepted by completion-check.

evidenceAssessment.description: Evaluator/model judgement that the attached hard evidence proves this testcase. Required passed TC-* rows should use verdict=proved; blocker/insufficient evidence must not be marked proved. To prevent self-proving model bias, a verdict=proved row MUST be backed by either a non-empty hardMetrics array (machine-checkable: exit code, build status, test counts, log/keyword match, screenshot hash, etc.) or a secondaryAssessment block (independent assessor verdict). manual_confirmed rows do not require those extra anchors.

assessor.description: Identifier of the primary assessor (model/agent/rule pack).

hardMetrics.description: Machine-checkable metrics that anchor verdict=proved. Each metric MUST be objectively verifiable from evidence files (exit codes, test counts, build status, log keyword presence, screenshot hash, geometry, OCR strings, etc.).

name.description: Stable metric name, e.g. exit_code, build_succeeded, tests_passed, log_keyword_matched, screenshot_hash_matched, ocr_text_present, geometry_match.

evidence.description: Path to the evidence file/log this metric was extracted from.

passed.description: Whether the metric satisfied its expectation. A proved verdict requires at least one metric with passed=true.

secondaryAssessment.description: Independent second-judge assessment that backs verdict=proved. The independent assessor MUST be distinct from the primary `assessor` (different model/agent/rule-pack identifier) so the verdict is not self-proved.

assessor.description: Identifier of the independent assessor; MUST differ from the primary assessor to count as independent.

independent.description: Optional explicit independence flag; when true, CodeAutonomy trusts the assessor as independent of the primary one.

assertionEvidenceTruncated.description: Set true when the value that proves this testcase's key assertion (e.g. a log payload field) is only observable inside a truncated/elided evidence line, so the full value was never actually captured. A verdict=proved row whose hardMetrics keyword/log/string/grep/regex/ocr value lands inside a truncated evidence line is rejected by completion-check (runtimeAssertionProofUntruncated=false) unless an independent secondaryAssessment or recorded humanConfirmation backs it. Prefer persisting the full untruncated log/screenshot and pointing the metric `evidence` at it instead of relying on a tail/excerpt.

evidenceReview.description: Deprecated alias for `evidenceAssessment`. Will be removed in a future release; emit a deprecation warning when normalized.

proof.description: Deprecated alias for `evidenceAssessment`. Will be removed in a future release; emit a deprecation warning when normalized.

uiExploration.description: Structured app-use / UI exploration trace for App/UI runtime testcases. Records goal-directed or user-path attempts so Evaluator can avoid repeating failed paths and completion can audit meaningful progress.

sourceUiMap.description: Path or object summarizing source-derived UI routes/selectors/data sources.

runtimeState.description: Path or object summarizing observed runtime hierarchy/screenshot/page state.

coverage.description: Completion-gate coverage summary derived from TestCases.md, Require.md, and testResults.

skippedChecks.description: Testcases intentionally not run in this iteration, usually because a dependency testcase failed (fail-fast) or a safe skip policy applied.

humanConfirmation.description: Human confirmation evidence used as the final fallback for visual/UI claims that measurable evidence and AI review cannot prove.

updatedAt.description: Last write time for the latest evaluation.json payload.

previousNextAction.description: Previous loop-control nextAction when CodeAutonomy explicitly consumed or normalized a route signal.

replanResolution.description: Audit metadata written only when the current loop explicitly consumes a replan signal after running Planner and a fresh workflow-check passes.
